title: glaucium corniculatum var. squamigerum
intro: Glaucium corniculatum var. squamigerum, also known as the poppy-spurred gromwell, is a type of flowering plant in the poppy family, Papaveraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to induce perspiration and reduce fever, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, including glaucine. The health benefits of Glaucium corniculatum var. squamigerum include its antispasmodic and expectorant properties, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Glaucium corniculatum var. squamigerum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
